pr.title: Matched if_2F72 (if_802F7E7C) by converting the while to a for loop
pr.author: DavidWeaverAudio

summary: Matched `if_802F7E7C` in `src/melee/if/if_2F72.c` by rewriting pointer-walking `do/while` code into an indexed `for` loop over six iterations. The logic still processes two adjacent GObj slots per iteration, calls `HSD_GObjPLink_80390228` when entries are non-null, and clears the backing table with `memzero(base, 0x34)`. decomp.dev reported one new match: `main/melee/if/if_2F72` item `if_802F7E7C`, moving from 93.12% to 100.00%.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; contains the matched `if_802F7E7C` loop rewrite.

what_changed: `if_802F7E7C` no longer declares and advances a separate `HSD_GObj** data` cursor.
The previous `do { ... i++; data += 2; } while (i < 6);` loop was replaced with `for (i = 0; i < 6; i++)`.
Accesses changed from cursor-relative `data[1]` and `data[2]` to base-relative indexed expressions `base[i * 2 + 1]` and `base[i * 2 + 2]`.
The function retained the same null checks and calls to `HSD_GObjPLink_80390228` for both candidate entries per iteration.
The PR added 8 lines and deleted 10 lines in one hunk.
smart_moves: Preserved semantics while changing loop expression shape, which is often enough to alter MWCC codegen for a match.
Used a single `base` pointer for both iteration and the final `memzero`, reducing alias/cursor state compared with separate `data` and `base` variables.
Made the fixed iteration count explicit in the `for` loop condition (`i < 6`), matching the apparent structure of a 6-entry pair traversal.
Kept the two-slot stride visible through `i * 2 + 1` and `i * 2 + 2`, avoiding manual pointer increment code that did not match.
decomp_lessons: For small fixed-count table traversals, try both cursor-style pointer increments and base-plus-index expressions; MWCC may prefer one loop shape for matching.
Converting a `do/while` to a `for` loop can change generated branch/setup code even when the logical iteration count is identical.
When a loop processes paired entries, representing the pair stride directly in array indexing (`i * 2 + offset`) may match better than incrementing the pointer by the stride.
If a function clears the original base pointer after iteration, keeping that base variable as the sole table reference can help avoid extra temporaries or mismatched register allocation.
naming_conventions: `if_802F7E7C` follows the project convention of address-derived names for still-unnamed functions.
`lbl_804A1340` remains an address-derived global label; the PR did not introduce a semantic name for it.
`base` was used as the stable pointer to the `lbl_804A1340` table.
`i` was used as the loop counter for the fixed six-iteration traversal.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed loop form from post-tested `do/while` to canonical pre-tested/counting `for` loop.
Removed a separately incremented pointer cursor (`data += 2`) and replaced it with computed indexed loads from `base`.
Maintained null-check-before-call structure around `HSD_GObjPLink_80390228` to preserve control flow.
Validated by decomp.dev: `if_802F7E7C` reached 100.00% for `main/melee/if/if_2F72`, with report showing +128 matched code bytes overall and +9 bytes for the item.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported one new match for `if_802F7E7C`.
follow_up_queries: Find other functions in `src/melee/if` that walk `lbl_804A1340` or similar GObj tables with pointer increments.
Search for unmatched fixed-count `do/while` loops that process two entries per iteration and try base-indexed `for` forms.
Check whether `lbl_804A1340` has enough known usage to receive a semantic global/table name in a future cleanup.
